module.title: ABC Electronics India - SAP TM End-to-End Implementation

topic.title: Lab 6: Customer Changes Delivery Date During Transit

learningObjectives.title: Assess in-transit customer change requests
learningObjectives.description: Evaluate operational and financial impact of revised delivery dates during shipment execution.

learningObjectives.title: Compare alternative execution strategies
learningObjectives.description: Analyze detention, temporary storage and return options based on network impact.

learningObjectives.title: Coordinate stakeholder approvals
learningObjectives.description: Drive aligned decisions across sales, transportation, carrier and warehouse teams.

learningObjectives.title: Update SAP TM execution accurately
learningObjectives.description: Record approved changes and create follow-on transportation actions for final delivery.

sections.title: Real-Time Lab 6: Customer Changes Delivery Date During Transit

sections.content: The replacement truck successfully resumed the journey after the breakdown in Lab 5. The shipment is now approaching Vellore when ABC Electronics receives an urgent call from Reliance Digital. Due to unexpected maintenance work at their Chennai warehouse, they cannot receive the shipment tomorrow morning as originally planned.

sections.title: Your Role
sections.content: You are the SAP TM Functional Consultant supporting the transportation control tower. You must determine the best business solution while minimizing additional logistics costs and maintaining customer satisfaction.

sections.title: Transportation Execution
rows: Committed to another shipment after delivery

sections.content: If the truck waits until the customer's new delivery date, it will miss its next scheduled assignment and the carrier will charge INR 45,000 for vehicle detention. Alternatively, the shipment can be unloaded into a temporary warehouse near Chennai and delivered later using a local distribution vehicle.

items: Coordinate with customer, carrier and warehouse.

sections.content: The Sales Director insists that the truck should wait because the customer is strategically important. However, the Transportation Manager points out that delaying the truck will also delay another shipment worth INR 4 Crores scheduled for the same vehicle.

sections.title: Consultant Evaluation
rows: Not if it impacts higher-priority business operations.
Should the Freight Order be updated?
Yes, execution details and events must reflect the change.

sections.title: Consultant Resolution
sections.content: Recommend unloading the goods into an approved temporary warehouse near Chennai. Update SAP TM with the revised execution events, create a follow-on local transportation requirement for final delivery, release the long-haul truck for its next assignment and notify all stakeholders of the revised plan.

sections.title: Consultant Best Practice
sections.content: Whenever a customer changes delivery requirements after shipment execution has started, evaluate the impact on the entire transportation network rather than optimizing only the affected shipment.

sections.title: Accenture Scenario Question
sections.content: A customer changes the delivery date while the shipment is already in transit. How would you respond? A strong consultant answer includes impact analysis, stakeholder communication, cost comparison, Freight Order updates and selection of the option that delivers the greatest overall business value.

items: Long-haul truck released for next assignment.
Project continues to Lab 7: Freight Invoice Mismatch & Freight Settlement.
